east-village.note: N/R/W from Union Sq or 8 St run direct to Herald Sq; the 6 now leaves a crosstown walk from Park Ave, so the L + 14 St transfer covers the Ave A end.

lower-east-side.note: F from 2 Av and M from Essex run direct to Herald Sq; J/Z need a transfer — and the stations still sit at the neighborhood's edges, so count the walk.

williamsburg.note: M direct to Herald Sq from the Marcy/Hewes side; L + 14 St transfer from Bedford. Ferry is still the scenic backup, not the fast one.

east-williamsburg.note: L + 14 St transfer is the default on a train already full when it reaches you; near the Flushing Av edge the M runs direct to Herald Sq and skips the transfer.

greenpoint.note: G to Court Sq, then E to Penn or 7 to Times Sq — the transfer is unavoidable, plan +10 min.

bushwick.note: M now runs direct to Herald Sq — the headline win; L or J/Z still mean a transfer, and the deeper into Bushwick you live, the longer the ride.

astoria.note: N/W direct to Herald Sq or Times Sq — same no-transfer elevated ride, then a short walk to 37th.

long-island-city.note: E drops you at Penn two blocks from the door; 7 to Times Sq and M to Herald Sq also run direct. The G covers your Brooklyn trips.

hells-kitchen.note: Now a 15-20 min walk from the upper 40s and 50s, or a one-stop A/C/E or 1 hop to Penn on rainy days.

yorkville.note: Q direct to Times Sq and Herald Sq is the play; the Lex 4/5/6 now mean the 42 St shuttle or a long crosstown walk.
